title: Next.js for business applications | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)

seo.title: Next.js for business applications | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
seo.description: Where Next.js makes sense for business applications, client portals, and products combining application logic with commercial content and discoverability.

page.title: Next.js for business applications | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
page.h1: Next.js for business applications, portals, and content-driven products
page.description: Where Next.js makes sense for business applications, client portals, and products combining application logic with commercial content and discoverability.

hero.title: Next.js is a practical tool, not a trend badge
hero.subtitle: It is strongest where content, SEO, first-load quality, and application logic need to coexist in one coherent stack.

intro: Next.js is a strong choice for projects that are more than a purely internal tool with no discoverability requirements.
For many business products it helps combine commercial pages, documentation, and application flows without splitting the stack unnecessarily.

sections.title: Where this technology is a strong fit
body: Technology should fit the product and delivery reality, not just developer preference.
bullets: commercial pages and product experience in one app
shared routing and architecture across content and app layers

sections.title: What to be careful about
body: A technology’s strengths disappear quickly when it is used in the wrong context or without a sound architecture.
bullets: using it where a simple SPA would do
adding complexity with no product benefit
weak data boundaries and rendering discipline

sections.title: Typical fit for a business project
body: The choice should support delivery speed, maintainability, and operational confidence over time.
bullets: business websites with commercial landing pages
business apps with content and discovery needs
products that benefit from both static and dynamic rendering

faq.question: Is Next.js useful for internal systems too?
faq.answer: Yes, but it is not always necessary. The key question is whether the project benefits from what it offers.

faq.question: Does Next.js automatically improve performance?
faq.answer: No. Final performance still depends on data flow, architecture, and implementation quality.

faq.question: Is it a good choice for a company website and app in one stack?
faq.answer: Yes. That is one of the contexts where it is often especially practical.

for: business websites with commercial landing pages
business apps with content and discovery needs
products that benefit from both static and dynamic rendering
notFor: using it where a simple SPA would do
adding complexity with no product benefit
weak data boundaries and rendering discipline
